mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and signals easier financial conditions, so equity valuation multiples expand and S&P 500 returns are positive over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not higher when FEDFUNDS is declining than when it is flat or rising, or if the sign of the effect is negative in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above recent levels marks labor-market deterioration and rising recession risk, which lowers expected corporate earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce below-baseline 20-day returns, or if more than 60% of triggered episodes are positive after the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: A strong monthly increase in RSAFS indicates improving consumer demand and retail revenue momentum, which supports near-term earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth turns positive after a prior 3-month contraction
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS reversals do not outperform the unconditional 5-day return distribution, or if the return effect disappears in consecutive post-contraction reversals.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO and PAYEMS both rise together, real activity is expanding through both output and payroll channels, confirming broad industrial recovery and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform either series alone, or if the combined signal fails to produce higher 20-day returns than the unconditional benchmark in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rising CPIAUCSL inflation trend combined with a still-low FEDFUNDS rate implies negative real policy rates, which supports risk assets through easier real financing conditions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if negative-real-rate periods do not show higher 60-day returns than positive-real-rate periods, or if the combined inflation-policy interaction has no incremental predictive power over either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in HOUST reflects strengthening housing demand and construction activity, which spills into consumer wealth and cyclicals, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases by at least 5% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if housing upswings do not lead to above-baseline 60-day returns, or if the sign is negative in repeated post-threshold samples.

mechanism_narrative: In a policy-sensitive contraction, rising PAYEMS with falling UNRATE signals labor-market healing that reduces default risk and lifts equity risk appetite, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months while PAYEMS rises
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-repair condition does not outperform single-variable triggers, or if triggered windows fail to generate positive excess 20-day returns relative to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: In the reopening phase, a rebound in INDPRO following severe pandemic disruption indicates supply normalization and operating leverage gains, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive months after a prior monthly drop of at least 3%
evidence_refs: fact:2020 industrial activity remains below pre-pandemic levels on the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if reopening-style industrial rebounds do not outperform the unconditional 5-day return distribution, or if the effect is absent once the prior drop threshold is met.
